data.description:
# Opportunistic detached drain for the Jira reconciler (mirror enrichment Tier-1)

## Context
Discovered while designing the cross-ticket overlap epic's async enrichment drain (only-crave-art). The Tier-1 pattern — a cheap opportunistic gate on ordinary invocations that DETACHES background work to a child surviving parent exit (reusing `src/rebar/_store/push.py:108` `Popen(start_new_session=True)`), with batch-cap + lenient lock + graceful skip — is general. The Jira reconciler (`reconcile`) currently relies on CI/manual runs; a comparable opportunistic drain could keep Jira in sync without requiring a dedicated CI job (many clients have none).

## Why an idea (undesigned)
Needs its own design pass: reconcile is heavier and outward-facing (mutates Jira), has stricter safety gates (`REBAR_MCP_ALLOW_JIRA_SYNC`, live-mode guards), rate/quota concerns, and different failure semantics than a local LLM enrichment. Whether an opportunistic detached reconcile is safe/desirable (vs. surprising outward writes from an incidental CLI call) is exactly the open question.

## Acceptance Criteria (to be designed)
- [ ] Decide whether an opportunistic detached reconcile is appropriate given its outward-facing, gated nature (or restrict to dry-run/bridge-fsck refresh).
- [ ] If pursued: reuse the enrichment Tier-1 drain machinery (gate + detach + batch-cap + lenient lock + graceful skip) rather than reimplementing.
- [ ] Respect existing reconcile safety gates; never perform live Jira sync without the explicit env authorization.
data.title: Opportunistic detached drain for the Jira reconciler (mirror enrichment Tier-1)
